title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on Types of Bonuses Under a Life Insurance Policy - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: reversionary bonus, terminal bonus, interim bonus, bajaj allianz life insurance, cash bonus, compound reversionary bonus
Body Content:
A bonus in any form is welcome, as you earn extra money. With regard to life insurance, a bonus represents an extra sum you can get, besides your base amount. In terms of life insurance, a bonus is any amount that gets amassed under the life insurance policy on an annual basis. It is payable when the policyholder dies, the policy is surrendered, or at the maturity of the policy, whichever of these takes place earlier. The bonus is a payout which extends over the applicable benefits in a life insurance plan. There are types of bonuses like reversionary bonus, terminal bonus, etc, but before you explore these, you should know under what policies you can get the bonuses.
Which Policies are Eligible for Bonuses?
All life insurance policies are not entitled to receive bonuses. Only those policies that are participating policies (“with-profit policies”) can qualify for any bonuses. Policyholders who hold such policies can benefit from the bonus payout through these policies. Participating policies are those that take part in the insurance company’s investment profits. Hence, policyholders get a payout, much like stockholders get a dividend. The bonus amount is not fixed, as it is dependent on the insurance company’s profits. Based on the investment income that an insurance company earns, the profits are shared with policyholders.
Reputed insurers like Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance declare bonuses that are shared with policyholders. Any bonus represents a percentage of the assured sum. A bonus is declared at the end of each financial year. Once a bonus is declared, it becomes a guaranteed payout. However, any insurance company reserves the right to determine the rate of the bonus. The policyholder may receive 90% of the earned profits, the remaining 10% being distributed among the company’s shareholders.
Types of Bonuses
For a participating life insurance plan that is entitled to give out bonuses, bonuses are classified into four major types:
Reversionary Bonus
Each policy’s allocated profits are paid by way of a reversionary bonus. This bonus adds some value to the amount totally payable to the nominee or the policyholder. Such a bonus is typically declared at the close of a financial year. However, it is only payable when a claim takes place. Commonly, two types of reversionary bonuses exist:
Simple Reversionary Bonus - This bonus is computed as a percentage of the assured sum. This bonus is declared as a calculation that is per thousand of the assured sum each year.
Compound Reversionary Bonus - Also calculated as a percentage rate, it applies to the assured sum, as well as all the accrued previous bonuses of the policy. Every year’s bonus is added to the assured sum, and the bonus of the next year is computed on that amount. With a compounded effect, the bonuses increase gradually.
Interim Bonus - The time that bonuses are declared is at the end of a financial year. However, in case the policy reaches maturity, or the policyholder’s death occurs between the bonus declaration dates, an interim bonus can be paid. In the case of an interim bonus, the calculation of the bonus is done for all the days remaining from the date of the last bonus.
Terminal Bonus - The terminal bonus is also called, as its name suggests, a final bonus. It is declared solely for those policies that attain their maturity dates. The terminal bonus is offered to policyholders for maintaining the policy till its date of maturity. Thus, the terminal bonus will not be applicable to policies that have been surrendered, or those that have obtained paid-up value.
Cash Bonus - The cash bonus that is declared and paid out for any policy will be applicable as a bonus in cash at the end of any given financial year. The cash bonus can be paid on an annual basis instead of at the maturity date of the policy.
Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Declares Bonus Worth Rs 1,070 Crore in FY22
One of the premier insurance companies in India, Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance, declared a whopping bonus in the March 2022 fiscal year ending. The insurer, uniquely, has declared a bonus consecutively for 21 years. The recent bonus is worth Rs. 1,070 crore, including a reversionary bonus of Rs. 840 crore, and a cash and terminal bonus amounting to Rs. 230 crore. The insurer announced that those participating policyholders’ policies, active for the complete assured sum as of 31 March 2022, are entitled to the bonus. For holders of such policies this translates to a kind of dividend similar to what shareholders receive as part of the profits of a company whose shares they have invested in.
The reversionary bonus that has been declared would be payable on the policy’s maturity, or in the event of the policyholder’s death, the insurer stated. The insurer has above 11.62 loyal and eligible policyholders, consistently trusting Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance to meet their financial goals. These are the customers who will be at an advantage with the announcement of the bonus. Additionally, the insurer stated that all bonuses would be generated from a surplus created by the participating policyholders’ funds.
Conclusion
The consecutive declaration of a bonus for each of the past 21 years, displays Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company’s relentless commitment to its customers, the MD and CEO of the company, Tarun Chugh, announced. From here, the organisation can only grow from strength to strength. This should compel potential life insurance plan seekers to sign on for a life insurance policy at the earliest.
